title: Tralfin

'''Tralfin''' was tropical resort [[Planet/Legends|world]] located in the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories']] [[Xappyh sector/Legends|Xappyh sector]]. In [[28 BBY/Legends|28 BBY]], the [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Qan Gindoch]] was [[Reproduction/Legends|born]] on Tralfin. Shortly after the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Battle of Yavin]], [[Moff/Legends|Moff]] [[Crin Harson]] of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] was [[Death/Legends|murdered]] on Tralfin. A group of [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]] agents with which Harson had been acquainted was implicated in the crime, but eventually the real killer was discovered and the Rebels were cleared of the murder charges.

==Description==
Tralfin was a lush, tropical paradise [[Planet/Legends|world]]<ref name="Murder" /> located in the [[Tralfin system/Legends|Tralfin system]], a part of the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories']] [[Xappyh sector/Legends|Xappyh sector]].<ref name="EAOC" /> It served as a luxury resort world and was owned and operated by an influential consortium of businessmen based in the [[Corporate Sector/Legends|Corporate Sector]].<ref name="Murder" />

==History==
The [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Smuggler/Legends|smuggler]] [[Qan Gindoch]] was [[Reproduction/Legends|born]] on Tralfin<ref name="WbC" /> in [[28 BBY/Legends|28 BBY]],<ref name="GindochDate">[[Wanted by Cracken (sourcebook)|''Wanted by Cracken'']] is dated to three [[Standard year/Legends|years]] after the [[Battle of Endor/Legends|Battle of Endor]]. Since ''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'' dates that conflict to [[4 ABY/Legends|4 ABY]], the placement of the sourcebook therefore translates to [[7 ABY/Legends|7 ABY]]. Furthermore, since ''Wanted by Cracken'' states that [[Qan Gindoch]] is age 35, he must have been [[Reproduction/Legends|born]] in [[28 BBY/Legends|28 BBY]].</ref> a fact which was noted<ref name="WbC" /> in [[7 ABY/Legends|7 ABY]]<ref name="GindochDate" /> by [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] [[General/Legends|General]] [[Airen Cracken/Legends|Airen Cracken]] in ''[[Wanted by Cracken]]'', a [[Datafile/Legends|datafile]] that served as a compilation of reports on various threats to the New Republic.<ref name="WbC" />

{{RPG|"[[Murder in Paradise]]"}}
Shortly after the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Battle of Yavin]],<ref name="CSWE">{{CSWECite|II|22|Harson, Moff Crin}}</ref> the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] [[Moff/Legends|Moff]] [[Crin Harson]] was present on Tralfin. He developed a friendly relationship with a group of<ref name="Murder" /> [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]] personnel<ref name="CSWE" /> who had also arrived on the resort world at that [[Time/Legends|time]]. Although Harson met with the Rebel agents in public, he did not reveal his position in the Empire to them.<ref name="Murder" />

Several [[Standard day/Legends|days]] after the Rebels' arrival on Tralfin, Harson was [[Death/Legends|murdered]].<ref name="Murder" /> The Moff's body was discovered in his rooms, and the nearby group of Rebels,<ref name="CSWE" /> which stumbled upon the murder scene several [[Standard minute/Legends|minutes]] after it was committed,<ref name="Murder" /> was implicated in the crime. Eventually, Harson's real killer was revealed, and the Rebel agents were cleared of all charges.<ref name="CSWE" />
{{Endgame}}

==Inhabitants==
Tralfin had an effective planetary [[Law enforcement agency/Legends|law enforcement agency]]. The world's judicial system was quick and harsh, with individuals guilty of murder being punished by death. During the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]], the Galactic Empire did not deem it necessary to station [[Troop/Legends|troops]] or weapons on Tralfin. As a result, to its visitors Tralfin seemed removed from the conflict. The world became a popular vacation site for personnel of the Rebel Alliance since they could be anonymous there.<ref name="Murder" />

==Behind the scenes==
Tralfin was created by [[Bill Olmesdahl]] as the setting for "[[Murder in Paradise]]," a [[Roleplaying game|roleplaying]] adventure hook published in the [[1992]] supplement ''[[Star Wars Gamemaster Screen]]'' for use with [[West End Games]]' ''[[Star Wars: The Roleplaying Game]]''. "Murder in Paradise" suggests several reasons for why the player characters would want visit the world, such as being rewarded by a luxury vacation by their superiors or a grateful client, or being sent on an undercover mission—possibly even to kidnap Moff Crin Harson, who ends up being murdered by another party.<ref name="Murder" />

[[File:Gamemaster_Screen.jpg|thumb|right|300px|Tralfin was used as a setting for a roleplaying adventure hook included in the ''Star Wars Gamemaster Screen''.]]
The adventure hook presents several options for the identity and motives of Harson's killer. It may be the Moff's [[Crin Harson's wife|wife]], who, unbeknownst to Harson, visits Tralfin and discovers his affair with a [[Crin Harson's mistress|mistress]], and subsequently kills her [[Marriage/Legends|husband]] in a fit of jealous rage. Other options include a grudge-bearing [[Imperial officer]] whose career Harson has ruined; Rebel Alliance sympathizers who carry out a [[Politics/Legends|political]] [[Assassin/Legends|assassination]] and then try to pin the blame on the player characters, who they believe are associated with the Galactic Empire; or a team of [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunters]] hired by one of Harson's superiors, who wishes to get rid of the power-hungry Moff.<ref name="Murder" />

"Murder in Paradise" also briefly discusses the potential first reaction of the player characters to Harson's murder, with both contacting Tralfin's law enforcement agency and attempting to hide the evidence of the crime being described as highly problematic for the characters. The adventure hook also suggests an alternate way to play it: instead of running a mystery adventure, the game-master player can frame it as a fast-paced escape scenario by having Tralfin's police launch a manhunt for the player characters. They then have to avoid capture and escape, and possibly have to attempt to prove their innocence in later adventures. This article assumes the scenario plays out as described.<ref name="Murder" />

The [[2008]] ''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' established the [[canon]]icity of several details of Moff Harson's murder on Tralfin with respect to the [[Star Wars Legends|''Star Wars'' Legends]] continuity. It established that the Moff was found dead in his own apartment,<ref name="CSWE" /> contradicting "Murder in Paradise," in which the body is discovered in the apartment of the individuals later implicated in his murder. This article assumes the latter source is correct.<ref name="Murder" /> The [[2009]] reference book ''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' placed the Tralfin [[Star system/Legends|system]], and therefore Tralfin itself, in grid square Q-3.<ref name="TEA" />
